methodsAttempted.query: site:uniroma2.it ("intelligenza artificiale" OR "IA generativa" OR ChatGPT OR "artificial intelligence") (linee guida OR policy OR didattica OR esami OR studenti OR ricerca) "Tor Vergata"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Tor Vergata Economics library GenAI pages plus course, news, and teaching-program leads, but no central university-wide AI policy page.
Firecrawl search found an official course page with course-level generative AI plagiarism wording and an Engineering library chatbot guide lead.
The central web.uniroma2.it robots.txt allows crawling, but the central search results reviewed here were news, program, or generic campus pages rather than AI policy guidance.
economia.uniroma2.it robots.txt allows the library and course paths used here, with Crawl-delay 10 and private/temp disallowed.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ingegneria.biblio.uniroma2.it Chatbot intelligenza artificiale fonti studio ricerca
methodsAttempted.notes: Official Engineering library WordPress page was fetched directly after discovery; robots.txt allows public posts and disallows only wp-admin.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found narrow public AI-related guidance from Tor Vergata library and course pages. No reliable public central university-wide AI policy was found in the reviewed official search results, so claims are scoped to the specific library or course source.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Corporate and Business Strategy - Bachelor of Science Business Administration & Economics
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: University Tor Vergata cherishes integrity of its students and faculty members. Any form of behavior which may be deemed unethical and or inappropriate for the academic community will be thoroughly sanctioned. Plagiarism and cheating are serious offenses and may be punished by failure on the exam, paper or project and eventually failure in the course. Usage of generative AI (including but not limited to ChatGPT) without a prior consent of the professor is considered plagiarism.

artifacts.claimText: The Tor Vergata Engineering library chatbot guide cautions that chatbots are not always reliable, may not disclose their answer sources, and may rely on outdated data; this is library guidance, not a university-wide AI tool policy.

artifacts.title: University of Rome Tor Vergata AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: The crawl found public official AI-related sources from Tor Vergata library and course pages, but no central public university-wide AI policy in the reviewed official search results. Staged claims are therefore limited to Economics library research guidance, BiblioAI literacy service language, one course-level generative-AI plagiarism rule, and Engineering library chatbot cautions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No central public university-wide AI policy was found in the reviewed official sources; evidence is scoped to the linked library or course pages.
The PRIMO beta service link was identified as credential-gated from the public source description and was not fetched.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
